title: jacksonia furcellata
intro: Jacksonia furcellata, also known as the prickly bush, is a shrub native to the eastern regions of Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Jacksonia furcellata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its usage should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with medications.
